name: Silas Murch
role: Chandler and black-market route broker, nominally neutral
publicFace: A stout, greasy-fingered candle-maker who sells information and shortcuts to anyone with coin.
privateGoal: To profit from tonight's chaos without committing to either side — he will sell the same back-alley route to a Patriot and a Redcoat if the timing works.
fear: Being left in the city when the fighting starts with no friends on either side.
knowledge: He knows a back-alley route from the North End to Union Street that avoids the Ann Street checkpoint entirely.
He has seen unusual troop movement toward the Common tonight and is already calculating what it means for his prices.
He knows Owen Pryce by face and has seen him near the garrison — he will sell this observation for the right price.
voice: Oily, cheerful, always angling — he phrases everything as a favor he is doing you.
trustLogic: He responds to coin, trade goods, or information he can resell. He is immune to appeals to patriotism or loyalty but will respond to fear — specifically, fear of what happens to collaborators after a war.
secrets: He has already been paid by Captain Aldrich to report any unusual movement through his alley tonight — he has not yet decided whether to honor this.
He has a cache of Patriot pamphlets hidden under his tallow vats that would hang him with the Loyalists.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: He names a price for everything and waits.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: He laughs and says he doesn't know anything worth knowing, then names a higher price.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not give Aldrich's name for free — that is his most valuable asset tonight.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If violence breaks out within sight of his shop.
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: He locks the shop door and disappears into the back — he has a bolt-hole under the floorboards.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: He sits down and pretends to have a bad leg.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: He immediately offers to tell everything he knows about the other side — whichever side that is.
